spec_match.rationale: Page runs script and renders dynamic UI, but the visible API surface (no direct API hint matched) only partially mirrors what the feature summary calls out — the headline behaviour isn't unambiguously demonstrated.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (0 buttons / 0 inputs / 1 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (chrome, complete, drag, dropeffect, each, effectallowed, event, reports, showing).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: References section cites the chromestatus.com/feature/<id> link directly.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 46 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the link between the demo and the spec's headline behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Confirm the on-screen interaction maps to a concrete API call from the feature summary on chromestatus: "The drag and drop specifications require the "dataTransfer"'s object "dropEffect" attribute to have certain predetermined values on "dragEnter", "dragOver" and "dragLeave". Drag enter and drag over sh". If the call is being shimmed or simulated, surface that fact in the page and add a real invocation behind a feature-detect.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("The drag and drop specifications require the "dataTransfer"'s object "dropEffect" attribute to have certain predetermined values on "dragEnter", "dragOver" and "dragLeave". Drag enter and drag over should have a "dropEffect" based on the cu") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/dnd.html#dndevents to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~2183 chars of script across 0 button(s) and 1 other control(s). hits 3 pass / 2 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
